revision.text: #redirect[[en:Three_Great_Festivals_of_Shikoku]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、四国地方で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の三つの祭を指す。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日～18日］ ==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。 この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。その頃からこの三つの祭を、四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
しかし四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のような全国的定着はまだしていない。 ==関連== *[[土居太鼓祭り]]（愛媛県[[四国中央市]]）［10月13日～15日］ [[category:日本の祭り|しこくさんだいまつり]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、四国地方で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の三つの祭を指す。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日～18日］ ==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。 この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。その頃からこの三つの祭を、四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
しかし四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のような全国的定着はまだしておらず、旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
「四国三大祭り」という言葉自体が、知名度の低い[[新居浜太鼓祭り]]の自慢をするときに、「他の二つと並ぶ有名な祭りだ」というやや誇張気味な文脈で使われることがほとんどである。 ==関連== *[[土居太鼓祭り]]（愛媛県[[四国中央市]]）［10月13日～15日］ [[category:日本の祭り|しこくさんだいまつり]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、四国地方で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の三つの祭を指す。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日～18日］ ==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。 この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。その頃からこの三つの祭を、四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
しかし四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
「四国三大祭り」という言葉自体が、知名度の低い[[新居浜太鼓祭り]]の自慢をするときに、「他の二つと並ぶ有名な祭りだ」というやや誇張気味な文脈で使われることがほとんどである。 ==関連== *[[土居太鼓祭り]]（愛媛県[[四国中央市]]）［10月13日～15日］ [[category:日本の祭り|しこくさんだいまつり]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、[[四国地方]]で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の三つの[[祭]]を指す。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日～18日］
==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。その頃からこの三つの祭を、四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
「四国三大祭り」という言葉自体が、知名度の低い[[新居浜太鼓祭り]]の自慢をするときに、「他の二つと並ぶ有名な祭りだ」というやや誇張気味な文脈で使われることがほとんどである。
[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[四国中央市]]の「[[土居太鼓祭り]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。
[[Category:日本の祭り|しこくさんだいまつり]]

[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[四国中央市]]の「[[土居太鼓祭り]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、[[四国地方]]で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の三つの[[祭]]を指す。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日～18日］
==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。その頃からこの三つの祭を、四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
「四国三大祭り」という言葉自体が、知名度の低い[[新居浜太鼓祭り]]の自慢をするときに、「他の二つと並ぶ有名な祭りだ」というやや誇張気味な文脈で使われることがほとんどである。
[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[新居浜市]]の「[[新居浜太鼓祭り]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。
== 関連項目 ==
*[[日本の祭り一覧]]
[[Category:日本の祭り|しこくさんだいまつり]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、[[四国地方]]で開催される特に有名で大きな以下の二つの[[祭]]と、当事者の祭りを含めていう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日～15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日～12日］
==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきたが、今でも知名度は二つには遠く及ばない。
==二大祭り・四大祭り== しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
いっぽう、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[西条市]]の「[[西条まつり]]」、愛媛県[[新居浜市]]の「[[新居浜まつり]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の三つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日〜15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日〜12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日〜18日］
==沿革== 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（[[昭和29年]]）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（[[昭和45年]]）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。また青年会議所が行った祭りの知名度に関するアンケートで、圧倒的な知名度の阿波踊り・よさこい祭りに次いで、大きくかけ離れるものの三位に新居浜太鼓祭りが入ったことで、この三つを四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
==二大祭り・四大祭り== しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
いっぽう、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[西条市]]の「[[西条まつり]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）は、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の三つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、昭和の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、平安時代もしくは鎌倉時代に始まりその後江戸時代で現在の原型になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、太平洋戦争終戦後の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体が昭和中期である。全国的に阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは有名であったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは[[1970年]]（昭和45年）の[[大阪府]]で行われた「[[日本万国博覧会]]」に出張派遣したのをきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになってきた。また青年会議所が行った祭りの知名度に関するアンケートで、圧倒的な知名度の阿波踊り・よさこい祭りに次いで、大きくかけ離れるものの三位に新居浜太鼓祭りが入ったことで、この三つを四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
== 二大祭り・四大祭り == しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のようには全国的定着がしていないだけではなく、当事者である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはない。旅行会社の広告などでは、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることも多い。
いっぽう、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「さぬき高松まつり」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[西条市]]の「[[西条まつり]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:名数3]]

いっぽう、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[西条市]]の「[[西条祭り|西条まつり]]」などが「四国四大祭りの一つ」を称している。

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の三つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、[[平安時代]]もしくは[[鎌倉時代]]に始まり、その後[[江戸時代]]で現在の原型ができがったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、[[室町時代]]後期もしくは[[江戸時代]]に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体がこの時期以降である。

== 二大祭り・四大祭り == しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のように全国的に定着していないだけではなく、当事県である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはなく、[[旅行会社]]の[[広告]]などでも、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることが多い。
一方で、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」、愛媛県[[新居浜市]]の「[[新居浜太鼓祭り|新居浜太鼓祭り]]」などは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]]

''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の三つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、[[伝成務天皇28年（158年]]に始まり、その後[[江戸時代]]で現在の原型ができがったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、[[室町時代]]後期もしくは[[江戸時代]]に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体がこの時期以降である。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的な知名度が上がったが、新居浜太鼓祭りは「[[日本の祭り]]」や銀座への出張派遣をきっかけに徐々に全国に知られるようになっていった。また[[青年会議所]]が行った祭りの知名度に関する[[アンケート]]で、圧倒的な知名度の阿波踊り・よさこい祭りに次いで、大きくかけ離れるものの、三位に新居浜太鼓祭りが入ったことで、この三つを四国三大祭と呼ぶようになった。
== 二大祭り・四大祭り == しかし、四国三大祭という呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のように全国的に定着していないだけではなく、当事県である愛媛以外の徳島・高知でもほとんど使われることはなく、[[旅行会社]]の[[広告]]などでも、阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることが多い。
一方で、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している。

一方で、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]]

== 沿革 == 四国三大祭という言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、[[室町時代中期]]に始まり、その後[[江戸時代]]で現在の原型ができがったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、[[室町時代]]後期もしくは[[江戸時代]]に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体がこの時期以降である。

一方で、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>

[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] ''' 四国三大祭り '''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の三つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］（一部地域は15日より開催） == 沿革 == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月}} 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、[[室町時代]]中期に始まり{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}、その後[[江戸時代]]で現在の原型ができがったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められた。よって、この三つの祭が揃ったこと自体がこの時期以降である。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きく影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて徐々に知られるようになっていった。（歴史や世論に加えて[[青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの三つを四国三大祭りと呼ぶ事となった。）
== 二大祭り・四大祭り == しかし、四国三大祭りという呼び方は、[[京都三大祭り]]や[[東北三大祭り]]のように全国的に定着していない為、[[旅行会社]]の[[広告]]などでは阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」とすることもある{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
一方で、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
愛媛県西条市では「我が西条祭りこそが四国三大祭り」だと話す市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

愛媛県西条市では「我が西条祭りこそが四国三大祭り」だと話す市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>

[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。

== 二大祭り・四大祭り == [[旅行会社]]の観光PRや[[広告]]などでは阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」として表記される事もある{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。

愛媛県西条市では「我が西条祭りこそが四国三大祭り」だと話す市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>

{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の3つの[[祭]]をいう。 * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］（一部地域は15日より開催）
== 沿革 == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月}} 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で一番歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りで、[[鎌倉時代]]に始まり{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}、その後[[江戸時代]]に現在の形態（[[太鼓台]]）になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。一番新しいのがよさこい祭りであり、[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められたとされている。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。
歴史や伝承、世論等に加えて[[青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの3つが四国三大祭りと呼ばれる様になった。
== 二大祭り・四大祭り == [[旅行会社]]の観光PRや[[広告]]などでは阿波踊り・よさこい祭りを「四国二大祭り」として表記される事もある{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などでは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
愛媛県[[西条市]]では「我が[[西条祭り]]こそが四国三大祭り」だと話す市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の3つの[[祭]]をいう。
（日付順に記載） * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］※一部地域は15日より開催
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で最も歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りであり、その礎となる新居郡祭礼の起源は[[平安時代]]とも云われ、[[江戸時代]]に現在の形（[[太鼓台]]）になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。よさこい祭りは[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められ、年々と盛大になり現在の賑わいを魅せるようになった。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。
歴史や伝承、世論等に加えて[[日本青年会議所|青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの3つが四国三大祭りと呼ばれる様になった。
== 四大祭り == 四国三大祭りの3つの祭りの他に、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などでは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
また、愛媛県の[[西条市]]内では「我が[[西条祭り]]こそが四国三大（四大）祭り」の一つだと話す市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]] * [[喧嘩祭り]] * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り]] * [[阿波踊り]] * [[よさこい祭り]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の3つの[[祭]]をいう。
（日付順に記載） * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］※一部地域は15日より開催
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で最も歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りであり、その礎となる新居郡祭礼の起源は[[平安時代]]とも云われ、[[江戸時代]]に現在の形（[[太鼓台]]）になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。よさこい祭りは[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められ、年々と盛大になり現在の賑わいを魅せるようになった。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。
歴史や伝承、世論等に加えて[[日本青年会議所|青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの3つが四国三大祭りと呼ばれる様になった。
== 四大祭り == ・四大祭りとしての根拠はなく、様々な諸説がある。一般的に4つ目の祭りは以下と言われている。
・四国三大祭りの3つの祭りの他に、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などでは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。
・愛媛県の[[西条市]]内では「我が[[西条祭り]]こそが四国三大（四大）祭り」の一つだと話す西条市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>

{{サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右}} '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり） refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: inserting initial edit on these so the draft system sends me talk messages instead of dummy user
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: inserting initial edit on these so the draft system sends me talk messages instead of dummy user
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]]

* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:夏休み]]

revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the **Niihama Taiko Festival**, which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is **Awa Odori**, believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the **Yosakoi Festival** was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], **Awa Odori** and **Yosakoi Festival** have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The **Niihama Taiko Festival**, known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: * **Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri** in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. * **Matsuyama Matsuri** in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the **Saijo Matsuri** should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ** '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ** '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: made a mistake with the dates
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ** '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ** '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]]
== See Also == [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:四国三大祭り]] * [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:高知県]] '''[[Category:new pages]] [[ja:よさこい祭り]]''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:福岡県]] '''[[Category:new pages]] [[ja:博多祇園山笠]]''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}''' [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:熊本県]] '''[[Category:new pages]] [[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]]''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:宮崎県]] '''[[Category:new pages]] [[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]]''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ロシア]].
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:夏休み]]

revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ** '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ** '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]]

The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri."

Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
