* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:夏休み]]

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to " == See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]] == See Also == <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:四国三大祭り]] * <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:高知県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:よさこい祭り]]''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:福岡県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:博多祇園山笠]]''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:熊本県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]]''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:宮崎県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]]''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ロシア]].
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:夏休み]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also! ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]] == See Also! == <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:四国三大祭り]] * <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:高知県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:よさこい祭り]]''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:福岡県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:博多祇園山笠]]''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:熊本県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]]''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:宮崎県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]]''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ロシア]].
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:夏休み]]

Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.

The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. '''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." [[Category:Completion tier 3]]== Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}} [[Category:日本の祭り|Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:三大祭り|Three Great Festivals]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|Culture of the Shikoku region]] == See Also == <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:四国三大祭り]] * <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:高知県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:よさこい祭り]]''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:福岡県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:博多祇園山笠]]''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:熊本県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:山鹿灯籠まつり]]''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:宮崎県]] '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:都城六月灯おかげ祭り]]''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:サバントゥイ]] – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ヴォルガ川]] in {{ill|Russia|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:ロシア]].
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:春祭り]] * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:秋祭り]] * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:冬祭り]] * {{ill|Tanabata|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:七夕]] * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:地域おこし]] * {{ill|Homecoming|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:帰省]] * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->[[Category:Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]][[ja:夏休み]]

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|12=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|12=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}}
== See Also == <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=draft}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> in {{ill|Russia|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->
*
<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->

revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|12=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|12=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|12=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}}
== See Also == <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=draft}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> '''<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> in {{ill|Russia|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Community building|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=draft}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->
* <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--> <!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku--><!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
<!-- Listed in chronological order --> * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|12=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|12=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}} in {{ill|Russia|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Community building|en}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}
*

<!--Three Great Festivals of Shikoku-->

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}} in {{ill|Russia|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Community building|en}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}
*

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:四国三大祭り up to rev 96777810
revision.text:
[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の3つの[[祭]]をいう。
<!--日付順に記載--> * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］※一部地域は15日より開催
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で最も歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りであり、その礎となる新居郡祭礼の起源は[[平安時代]]とも云われ、[[江戸時代]]に現在の形（[[太鼓台]]）になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。よさこい祭りは[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められ、年々と盛大になり現在の賑わいを魅せるようになった。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。
歴史や伝承、世論等に加えて[[日本青年会議所|青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの3つが四国三大祭りと呼ばれる様になった。
== 四大祭り == * 四大祭りとしての根拠はなく、様々な諸説がある。一般的に4つ目の祭りは以下と言われている。 * 四国三大祭りの3つの祭りの他に、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などでは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 * 愛媛県の[[西条市]]内では「我が[[西条祭り]]こそが四国三大（四大）祭り」の一つだと話す西条市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]] * [[喧嘩祭り]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り]] === 四国三大祭り === * [[よさこい祭り]] * [[阿波踊り]] * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[四国三大祭り]] to [[Three Great Festivals of Shikoku]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[画像:Awa Odori Dancers.JPG|サムネイル|280px|阿波踊り|右]] '''四国三大祭り'''（しこくさんだいまつり）とは、一般的に[[四国地方]]で開催される知名度の高い以下の3つの[[祭]]をいう。
<!--日付順に記載--> * [[よさこい祭り]]（[[高知県]][[高知市]]）［8月9日 - 12日］ * [[阿波踊り]]（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]、[[鳴門市]]、[[三好市]]他）［8月12日 - 15日 他］ * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]（[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]）［10月16日 - 18日］※一部地域は15日より開催
== 沿革 == 四国三大祭りという言葉で呼ばれ始めたのは、[[昭和]]の中後期以降である。この三つの祭で最も歴史が古いとされているのは新居浜太鼓祭りであり、その礎となる新居郡祭礼の起源は[[平安時代]]とも云われ、[[江戸時代]]に現在の形（[[太鼓台]]）になったとされている。次に古いのが阿波踊りで、室町時代後期もしくは江戸時代に始まったとされている。よさこい祭りは[[太平洋戦争]][[終戦]][[戦後|後]]の[[1954年]]（昭和29年）に阿波踊りに負けないものを作ろうと始められ、年々と盛大になり現在の賑わいを魅せるようになった。
[[マスメディア]]への露出度から、阿波踊りとよさこい祭りは全国的に知名度が上がり、類似する全国各地の踊り（催し物・イベント）にも大きな影響を与えている。新居浜太鼓祭りは歴史ある神事である事や喧嘩祭りを維持するために観光化に反対する地元民も多い中、大阪万博や国体開会式、海外派遣事業などを通じて全国的に知られるようになっていった。
歴史や伝承、世論等に加えて[[日本青年会議所|青年会議所]]が行った祭り知名度に関する[[アンケート]]の上位となったこの3つが四国三大祭りと呼ばれる様になった。
== 四大祭り == * 四大祭りとしての根拠はなく、様々な諸説がある。一般的に4つ目の祭りは以下と言われている。 * 四国三大祭りの3つの祭りの他に、[[香川県]][[高松市]]の「[[さぬき高松まつり]]」、愛媛県[[松山市]]の「[[松山まつり]]」などでは「四国四大祭りの一つ」と称している{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 * 愛媛県の[[西条市]]内では「我が[[西条祭り]]こそが四国三大（四大）祭り」の一つだと話す西条市民も多い{{要出典|date=2016年6月}}。 <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|よさこい祭り Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|新居浜太鼓祭り Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|さぬき高松まつり </gallery>
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の祭一覧]] * [[喧嘩祭り]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り]] === 四国三大祭り === * [[よさこい祭り]] * [[阿波踊り]] * [[新居浜太鼓祭り]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しこくさんたいまつり}} [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:三大祭り]] [[Category:四国地方の文化|さんたいまつり]]

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}} in {{ill|Russia|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Community building|en}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}
*
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri}}

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]

Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:四国地方の文化]] from page
revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}} in {{ill|Russia|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
* {{ill|Spring Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Autumn Festivals|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Winter Festivals|en}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Community building|en}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en}}
*

The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|qq=}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo City|qq=}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosakoi Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi City|qq=}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|qq=}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo City|qq=}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosakoi Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosakoi Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kochi City|ja|高知市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Prefecture|qq=}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City|qq=}}, {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyoshi City|ja|三好市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi City|qq=}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Niihama City|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|qq=}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu City|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama City|ja|松山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}}. * In {{ill|Saijo City|ja|西条市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo City|qq=}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosakoi Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosakoi Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Awa Odori|ja|阿波踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Haru Matsuri|lt=Haru Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Aki Matsuri|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Fuyu Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''Yosakoi Festival''' was created in [[1954]] (Showa 29) after [[太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''Yosakoi Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|qq=}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. * In [[:en:Saijō, Ehime|Saijo City]], some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
