<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11419692]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Shikoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kochi City]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]]) [August 9 - 12] * [[:en:Awa Dance Festival|Awa Odori]] ([[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]], [[:en:Miyoshi, Tokushima|Miyoshi City]], and other areas in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ([[:en:Niihama|Niihama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in [[:en:1954|1954]] (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. * In [[:en:Saijō, Ehime|Saijo City]], some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Awa Dance Festival|Awa Odori]] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}
*

''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. * In [[:en:Saijō, Ehime|Saijo City]], some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11419692]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Shikoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kochi City]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kochi Prefecture]]) [August 9 - 12] * [[:en:Awa Dance Festival|Awa Odori]] ([[:en:Tokushima (city)|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Naruto, Tokushima|Naruto City]], [[:en:Miyoshi, Tokushima|Miyoshi City]], and other areas in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ([[:en:Niihama|Niihama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in [[:en:1954|1954]] (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. * In [[:en:Saijō, Ehime|Saijo City]], some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Awa Dance Festival|Awa Odori]] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11419692]] Wikidata name: Three Great Festivals of Shikoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori}} ({{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}}, {{ill|Naruto, Tokushima|en|Naruto, Tokushima|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City}}, {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|WD=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ({{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama City}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in {{ill|1954|en|1954|WD=Q18581|lt=1954}} (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in {{ill|1954|en|1954|WD=Q18581|lt=1954}} (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.

However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.

<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]]
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori}} ({{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City}}, {{ill|Naruto, Tokushima|en|Naruto, Tokushima|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City}}, {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|WD=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ({{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama City}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in {{ill|1954|en|1954|WD=Q18581|lt=1954}} (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四国三大祭り]]
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori|1=Awa Dance Festival}} ({{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|Naruto, Tokushima|en|Naruto, Tokushima|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|WD=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Tokushima}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ({{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama City|1=Niihama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in {{ill|1954|en|1954|WD=Q18581|lt=1954|1=1954}} (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City|1=Saijō, Ehime}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
* [[日本の祭一覧|List of festivals in Japan]] * [[喧嘩祭り|Fighting Festival]] * [[日本三大喧嘩祭り|Japan's Three Great Fighting Festivals]]
=== Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri === * {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori|1=Awa Dance Festival}} * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}}
== See Also ==
* '''''' * ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Yamaga Lantern Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Lantern Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * ''' {{ill|Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakonojo Rokugatsudo Okage Festival|qq=}}''' '''''' * {{ill|Sabantuy|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sabantuy|qq=}} – A summer festival held along the middle reaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Volga River|qq=}} in {{ill|Russia|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russia|qq=}}.
* {{ill|Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Matsuri|lt=Spring Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Autumn Matsuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Matsuri|lt=Winter Festivals|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Winter Festivals|qq=}} * {{ill|Tanabata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|qq=}} * {{ill|Community building|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Community building|qq=}} * {{ill|Homecoming|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homecoming|qq=}} * {{ill|Summer Vacation|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Vacation|qq=}}

The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City|1=Saijō, Ehime}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Three Great Festivals of Shikoku''' refers to the three most prominent and well-known festivals held in the [[:en:四国地方|Shikoku region]] of Japan.
* {{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kochi City|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}) [August 9 - 12] * {{ill|Awa Dance Festival|en|Awa Dance Festival|WD=Q1185363|lt=Awa Odori|1=Awa Dance Festival}} ({{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|Naruto, Tokushima|en|Naruto, Tokushima|WD=Q460812|lt=Naruto City|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|WD=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Tokushima}}, and other areas in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}) [August 12 - 15, among other dates] * {{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|1=Niihama Taiko Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501940}} ({{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama City|1=Niihama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}) [October 16 - 18, with some areas starting on the 15th]
== History == The term "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri" began to be used from the mid to late [[:en:昭和|Showa period]]. Of these three festivals, the oldest is said to be the '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', which originated as the Niihama District Festival during the [[:en:平安時代|Heian period]] and evolved into its current form (with {{ill|taikodai|ja|太鼓台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taikodai|qq=}}) during the [[江戸時代|Edo period]]. Next is '''Awa Odori''', believed to have started in the late [[室町時代|Muromachi period]] or the Edo period. Lastly, the '''YOSAKOI Festival''' was created in {{ill|1954|en|1954|WD=Q18581|lt=1954|1=1954}} (Showa 29) after [[:en:太平洋戦争|World War II]] to rival the grandeur of Awa Odori and has since grown into the vibrant spectacle it is today.
Due to their exposure in [[マスメディア|mass media]], '''Awa Odori''' and '''YOSAKOI Festival''' have achieved nationwide recognition, influencing similar dances and events across Japan. The '''Niihama Taiko Festival''', known for its historical significance and as a "fighting festival," has faced resistance from locals against overt commercialization. However, it gained national recognition through events such as the [[:en:大阪万博|Osaka Expo]], the [[国体|National Sports Festival of Japan]], and international cultural exchanges.
The combination of history, tradition, and popularity, along with surveys conducted by organizations such as the {{ill|Junior Chamber International Japan|ja|日本青年会議所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Chamber International Japan|1=Junior Chamber International Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509400}}, established these three festivals as the "Shikoku San-Dai Matsuri." == Shikoku Four Major Festivals == * Although there is no definitive basis, various claims suggest additional festivals as part of "Shikoku Yon-Dai Matsuri" (Four Major Festivals). Common candidates include: ''' '''Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri''' in {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City|1=Takamatsu}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. ''' '''Matsuyama Matsuri''' in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. * In {{ill|Saijō, Ehime|en|Saijō, Ehime|WD=Q633945|lt=Saijo City|1=Saijō, Ehime}}, some locals argue that the '''Saijo Matsuri''' should be one of the three or four major festivals of Shikoku.
<gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> Image:Yosakoi Performers at Kochi Yosakoi 2006 20.jpg|{{ill|YOSAKOI Festival|ja|よさこい祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YOSAKOI Festival|qq=}} Image:Niihama Taiko Festival - battle.jpg|{{ill|Niihama Taiko Festival|ja|新居浜太鼓祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niihama Taiko Festival|qq=}} Image:Japan - Takamatsu Awa Odori Bon Festival 03.jpg|Sanuki Takamatsu Matsuri </gallery>
